summary: Reformatted the repository root build object list in `obj_files.mk` by splitting one long `TEXT_O_FILES :=` continuation into multiple `TEXT_O_FILES +=` blocks separated by blank lines. The PR body states the motivation was to make the file better for git, vim, readability, and similar tooling. No object entries are evidenced as added or removed for decompilation progress; the change is primarily a maintainability/readability refactor of the build manifest.

key_files.role: Only changed file; reformatted `TEXT_O_FILES` object list into multiple appended sections.

what_changed: Changed `obj_files.mk` formatting from a single large `TEXT_O_FILES :=\` chain with blank continuation separators into repeated `TEXT_O_FILES +=\` chunks.

Inserted line breaks and new `TEXT_O_FILES +=` declarations between logical groups such as lb, cm, pl, mp, ef, fighter/chara groups, gm, gr, db, mn, item/interface, Runtime, MSL, MetroTRK, dolphin libraries, and sysdolphin baselib.
Preserved the object-list style using `$(BUILD_DIR)/...` paths and `.c.o` / `.s.o` outputs while making individual groups easier to edit.
The diff reports 144 added lines and 18 deleted lines in one file, consistent with reformatting rather than a content-heavy source change.
smart_moves: Used Make's additive assignment, `TEXT_O_FILES +=`, to break a very long continuation list into independently editable chunks without changing the variable's accumulated meaning.
Separated object groups with actual blank lines instead of escaped blank continuation lines, improving readability and making future diffs smaller and easier to review.
Kept the original object ordering visible within groups, which matters for build/link order in decompilation projects even when the PR itself is just formatting.
Targeted a high-churn central manifest file where small formatting improvements can reduce merge conflicts and editor pain for future source/asm migrations.
decomp_lessons: For decomp repositories with large ordered object manifests, prefer readable append blocks over one monolithic escaped variable definition when Make semantics allow it.
Maintaining object order remains important; formatting-only build manifest PRs should avoid reordering unless explicitly intended and justified.
Breaking object lists at subsystem or character boundaries makes later decomp PRs easier to inspect because adding or replacing `.s.o` with `.c.o` affects a smaller local section.
Repository hygiene changes can be valuable even when they do not improve match percentage directly, because they reduce friction in future matching and file-splitting work.
naming_conventions: `TEXT_O_FILES` is the root build variable collecting text-section object files.
Object entries consistently use `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/.../*.c.o` for compiled C sources and `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/.../*.s.o` for assembly stubs.
The manifest groups paths by broad subsystem directories such as `melee/ft`, `melee/gm`, `melee/gr`, `Runtime`, `MSL`, `MetroTRK`, `dolphin`, and `sysdolphin`.

Fighter character directories retain mixed existing naming conventions such as `ftDonkey`, `ftMario`, `ftCaptain`, `ftNess`, `ftMars`, and lowercase assembly stub names like `ftmario.s.o` or `ftdonkey_2.s.o`; this PR did not rename them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was introduced.
The build-manifest tactic is to use multiple `TEXT_O_FILES +=` append blocks so future replacements of `asm/.../*.s.o` with `src/.../*.c.o` can be made in localized, reviewable chunks.
Because object ordering is retained, this kind of formatting can support future matching work without intentionally perturbing link order.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
The only stated rationale from the PR body was: "Better for git, vim, readability, etc."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `obj_files.mk` after this formatting change to see whether append blocks reduced diff noise.
Search for conventions around preserving object order when converting `asm/*.s.o` entries to `src/*.c.o` entries.
Look for subsequent build-system refactors that further split or generated `obj_files.mk`.
